mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and yields negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and industrial production confirms broad-based economic strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 65% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation during a monetary tightening cycle forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing market volatility and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in 60% of high-inflation observations during tightening periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows, or positive production growth correlates with negative equity returns in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.2 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and consumer confidence, which reduces downstream economic activity and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in 75% of cases when HOUST breaches the 1200 threshold, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in 60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth below 1.5% during a late-cycle expansion indicates weakening aggregate demand, which compresses corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 65% of low-GDP-growth quarters, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in a walk-forward validation framework.
